title: Write user documentation
description: README with quick start, full documentation covering all features, example configurations for each supported agent.

title: Phase 3: Core Implementation
description: Build the core dispatcher: event routing, script discovery, ordering, and termination control.

title: hooks-dispatch: Unix-style hook router for AI agents
description: Build a generic, file-system-based dispatch system that routes hook events from AI coding agents to downstream scripts with ordering and termination control. See README.md for full spec.

title: Document Claude Code hook system
description: Research and document all hook events Claude Code supports, configuration format, and invocation semantics.
close_reason: Documented 17 hook events, configuration format, handler types, and integration approach

title: Implement termination control
description: Handle script exit codes and signals. Support continue-on-error and stop-on-failure modes. Propagate appropriate exit status.

title: Phase 4: Agent Integration
description: Integrate with specific AI coding agents: Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, etc.

title: Implement script discovery
description: Build file-system scanner that discovers scripts in hooks.d directories. Parse ordering prefixes, validate executability, build execution plan.

title: Phase 5: Polish & Distribution
description: Graph visualization, documentation, and cross-platform binary distribution.

title: Research existing hook managers
description: Search for existing solutions: hook dispatchers, event routers, or similar tools. Document what exists and gaps our tool would fill.
close_reason: Validated no existing solution; identified patterns to adopt from Lefthook, NetworkManager, run-parts

title: Document Copilot hook system
description: Research GitHub Copilot's extensibility points, hook events, and configuration options.
close_reason: Documented 8 hook events, JSON config in .github/hooks/, preToolUse blocking, CLI support

title: Implement CLI interface
description: Build CLI with subcommands: dispatch <event>, list, graph, version. Handle args, config paths, verbosity.

title: Define hooks.d directory convention
description: Specify the directory structure for hook scripts: naming conventions (e.g., 01-lint.sh, 02-test.sh), subdirectory organization per event type, and discovery rules.
close_reason: Defined hooks.d/ structure, event directories, numeric ordering, script interface, manifest format

title: Research: 'ask' decision behavior across agents
description: Investigate how each agent (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot) handles the 'ask' decision from hooks. Questions: Does the hook re-run? Is approval context passed? Can we standardize? May require agent feature requests. Block implementing 'ask' support until this is understood.
close_reason: Users can implement approval flows in their hooks (Slack, webhooks, etc). Not our concern.

title: Spec graph visualization requirements
description: Define what the hook graph should show: event sources, script chains, dependencies, execution order. Output formats (terminal, DOT, SVG?).
close_reason: Specified terminal/DOT/JSON formats, validation warnings, phased implementation

title: ADR: Go as implementation language
description: Document decision to use Go for implementation. Consider: cross-compilation, single binary, ecosystem, alternatives (Rust, Crystal, bash).
close_reason: ADR accepted: Go for cross-platform binary, fast startup, mature ecosystem

title: Phase 1: Research & Discovery
description: Research existing solutions, document agent hook systems, and make foundational decisions before implementation.
close_reason: All research tasks complete: existing solutions, Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, Junie, daemon vs CLI

title: Setup cross-platform builds
description: Configure Go build for Linux, macOS, Windows. Set up GitHub Actions for release binaries. Document installation methods.

title: Decide: daemon vs CLI model
description: Evaluate trade-offs between persistent daemon (always listening) vs CLI invocation (called per event). Consider: startup latency, resource usage, complexity, reliability.
close_reason: Recommended hybrid CLI model: start with pure CLI, add optional daemon later. Go startup acceptable for AI agent hook frequency.

title: Phase 2: Architecture & Design
description: Define conventions, semantics, and architecture. Produce ADRs for key decisions.
close_reason: All design specs complete: directory convention, termination control, Go ADR, graph visualization

title: Implement event dispatcher
description: Core routing logic: receive event name, find matching hooks.d subdirectory, execute scripts in order, handle termination control.

title: Implement init command with agent setup
description: Add 'hooks-dispatch init --agent=claude|cursor|copilot' command that: 1) Creates hooks.d/ structure, 2) Configures agent to use hooks-dispatch, 3) Hoists existing hooks into hooks.d/, 4) Provides safe defaults

title: Design termination control semantics
description: Define behavior for script failures: continue-on-error, stop-on-failure, exit codes, signals. How do scripts communicate termination intent?
close_reason: Defined exit codes (0/1/2), stop-on-failure vs continue-on-error, timeout handling, decision aggregation

title: Claude Code integration
description: Configure hooks-dispatch as Claude Code's hook handler. Document setup, test with real hooks, provide example config.

title: Implement graph visualization
description: Build 'hooks-dispatch graph' command that outputs visual representation of hook chains. Support terminal and DOT format output.

title: Document Cursor hook system
description: Research and document Cursor's hook/extension system, events supported, and configuration format.
close_reason: Documented 13+ lifecycle events, JSON config format, plugin system, and Claude Code compatibility

title: Cursor integration
description: Integrate hooks-dispatch with Cursor's hook system. Document setup and provide example configuration.

title: Document Junie hook system
description: Research JetBrains Junie AI agent's hook/extension system. Find events, configuration, and integration points.
close_reason: No traditional hooks - uses MCP/Allowlist model. Limited integration opportunity, recommend skipping for now.
